name: Oka (Pyraminx)
description: An advanced Pyraminx method developed by Yusuf Oka. The Oka method focuses on solving a top layer (or block) first, then using optimized algorithms to finish the remaining edges in one look. It is designed for high-level Pyraminx speedcubing and emphasizes efficient blockbuilding and low move counts. The method allows for excellent lookahead and planning during inspection.

steps.name: Orient Tips
steps.description: Twist all four tips to match their adjacent centers. Trivial step done at any point.
tips: Handle tips whenever convenient during the solve
Can be incorporated during other steps

steps.name: Solve Top
steps.description: Solve the top layer by building a complete top with all three edges correctly placed and oriented around the top tip. This creates a fully solved top face and the three edges connecting to it.
tips: Plan the entire top during inspection
Be color-neutral to find the easiest top to solve
Use efficient move sequences — avoid unnecessary turns

steps.name: Solve Bottom Edges
steps.description: Solve the three remaining bottom-layer edges using algorithms. Since the top is fully solved, the bottom edges need to be cycled and/or flipped into position. This step has a small number of cases that can be solved in one look.
tips: Recognize bottom edge cases quickly by checking orientations
Only a handful of cases exist for three bottom edges
Practice transitioning smoothly from top solve to bottom algorithms

pros: Full top can be planned in inspection
Highly competitive at the top level
cons: Top layer must be fully solved, which can be harder than building a V
creator: Yusuf Oka
